QGIS Processing documentation: 'Distance to nearest hub (line to hub)' creates lines joining source points to nearest destination features. Verified in export_result.sh line 57: geometry type check for 'LineString'

setup_task.sh line 38: Feature with name 'SFO Cargo' has coordinates [-122.38, 37.62]

privileged_info_summary: The task uses fully synthetic data generated by setup_task.sh. It contains two warehouse hubs (Oakland Port at [-122.3, 37.8] and SFO Cargo at [-122.38, 37.62]) and six retail stores (SF Downtown, Berkeley, San Mateo, Daly City, Hayward, and Redwood City) created as GeoJSON point features. The expected output is six LineString features connecting each store to its nearest warehouse, created by QGIS's standard 'Distance to nearest hub (line to hub)' Processing tool. All metadata claims about file paths, feature count, geometry type, and warehouse coordinates have been verified against the setup and verification scripts.
